Blog / Article

Migrating to v3: What Changed

March 8, 2026 · DevRef Team

DevRef v3 is a major release that rethinks how configuration, routing, and plugins work together. We've simplified the config format, moved to file-based routing by default, and introduced a new plugin lifecycle that gives authors much more control over the build pipeline.

The biggest change is the new devref.config.ts format. In v2, configuration was spread across multiple files. Now everything lives in a single typed config file with full IntelliSense support. The migration is mostly mechanical — we've built a codemod that handles 90% of cases automatically.

// Run the migration tool
$ npx devref migrate v3
// Review and commit the changes
$ git diff
$ npm run dev

We've also overhauled the routing layer. Dynamic routes now use a bracket syntax ([slug].tsx) that maps directly to URL segments. Catch-all routes, optional parameters, and route groups are all supported natively — no more manual route configuration.

Upgrade guide
See the full v3 migration docs for a complete list of breaking changes and automated fixes.
{{footer}}
